decisions.reasoning: Iron Hands threatens an OHKO with Wild Charge, and Tornadus-Therian cannot secure a KO. Switching to Flutter Mane provides an immunity to Iron Hands' primary STAB moves and forces the opponent to respect a faster, high-damage threat.
Flutter Mane is already fainted (0% HP), so I must switch to a healthy Pokemon. Tornadus-Therian is the safest pivot to scout the Iron Hands' next move while resisting its Fighting-type coverage.

Tornadus-Therian is threatened with an OHKO by Iron Hands' Wild Charge. Switching to Incineroar provides an Intimidate drop to mitigate physical damage and offers a safer pivot point against Iron Hands.

High Horsepower is a 2HKO and deals significantly more damage than Glacial Lance due to the type advantage against Iron Hands. Since Iron Hands is running Life Orb and cannot OHKO Calyrex-Ice, I will secure the 2HKO before the opponent can recover HP or switch.
Calyrex-Ice is at 14% HP and will be KO'd by any attack from Flutter Mane. Since Glacial Lance is a guaranteed OHKO, I must take the shot now to remove the threat before Calyrex-Ice faints.
Calyrex-Ice has fainted, so I must send in a new Pokemon. Incineroar is the best choice to pivot into Flutter Mane to threaten it with Intimidate and potential Knock Off or Flare Blitz pressure.
Knock Off removes Flutter Mane's Booster Energy, significantly weakening its offensive presence and utility for the rest of the match while dealing meaningful chip damage.

Tornadus-Therian is in 2HKO range of Flutter Mane's Moonblast and is slower, making it a liability to stay in. Switching to Incineroar provides a safe pivot to handle the special attack and potentially threaten with Knock Off or Intimidate support.
Tornadus-Therian is at 23% and will be KO'd by Flutter Mane's Moonblast or Shadow Ball. Switching to Incineroar allows me to tank the special hit and potentially pivot or threaten with Knock Off.
